title: Tanacetum zahlbruckneri
intro: Tanacetum zahlbruckneri, also known as Zahlbruckner's tansy, is a perennial plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of anxiety, digestive issues, and menstrual cramps. The bioactive constituents of Tanacetum zahlbruckneri include sesquiterpene lactones, particularly tanacetone and chamissonobtanol, as well as flavonoids and phenolic acids. Tanacetum zahlbruckneri is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use is largely limited to traditional folk medicine due to a lack of modern scientific research.
